judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 substantively satisfies all four expectations (traceable golden-diff review, re-measured flaky/lint figures, reproducibility check) while Report 1 only satisfies the live-test-cost expectation and otherwise treats docs/testing.md numbers as authoritative and skips diff/reproducibility verification.

report_1.evidence: The deselected tests are the two live tests that make real API calls and incur per-call cost... Enabling live tests would unnecessarily incur API costs without validating anything about the weight change.

report_2.evidence: The live tests in `test_live_model.py` hit a real API endpoint and incur per-call cost. Per the project documentation project documentation: "Gate paid or costly test suites behind an explicit switch" — they should remain opt-in, deselected by default.

Per project documentation: before committing a recaptured fixture, read the diff and confirm every changed hunk traces to your intended change. Run `scripts/update_golden.py` to regenerate the fixture. Then inspect the diff: verify each score change corresponds to the weight rebalance (item-2 score decreased because coverage weight dropped; item-1 and item-3 increased because precision weight rose).

report_1.evidence: Source these numbers from `docs/testing.md`, which explicitly documents the current test suite status. This is the authoritative source for test health metrics in the project.

report_2.evidence: Do NOT cite stale numbers from docs/testing.md. Measure yourself... Do not cite the "9 lint warnings" or "2 flaky tests" from docs/testing.md—those figures are outdated (actual counts are 4 and 1 respectively).
Fixture determinism | ✓ Verified | Re-ran test twice; consistent results ... Run test suite a third time to confirm determinism.
